free_text: Isomeric cross section ratio for the formation of
Se-73-m,g in various nuclear processes.
Details of
experiment.
-Description
of uncertainties
The Julich compact cyclotron CV-28
For 28-MeV alpha particles or 36-MeV He3 particles
The isochronous cyclotron for 45-MeV
protons and 56-MeV deutrons
The total errors in the absolute cross sections
for the formation of the ground state were about 16%.
The errors in the isomeric cross section ratios ranged
between 10 and 20%, the energy degradation calculation
in the stacked foils also contained some error.  It was
about +-0.5-MeV up to a projectile energy of 15-MeV and
about +-0.3 MeV at higher energies.
Description of the main partial uncertainties taken
from A0467 (see REL-REF):
The error in the thickness of each foil
The error in the beam current measurement.
Absolute activity measurement of the products.
Statistical model calculations. Investigations
on the Se-74(n,2n) reaction were performed.
Last checking has been done.
SD: 'COREL' added to STATUS according  to
remark from Prof.S.M.Qaim. ERR-ANALYS updated.
STATUS moved to Subents 002-008.
Thin samples were prepared by electrolytic deposition
of Ge on copper backing.
-By authors* measurement of the 7.1-hr Se-73-g activity
via gamma-ray spectrometry was relatively
straight forward. The excitation function of the
Ge-70(alpha,n)Se-73-g reaction could be determined in
absolute terms since in the investigated energy range
no other reaction contributions.
Fig.2 from Phys.Rev.,C38(1988)645
SD: STATUS added.
Thin samples were prepared by electrolytic deposition
of Ge on copper backing.
- By authors.  Measurement of Se-73-m in alpha particle
induced reactions on Ge presented difficulty due to the
strong  copper matrix activity.  After irradiation each
electroplated foil was therefore treated with 3
milliliters warm 3% hydrogen  peroxide.  The very thin
layer of Ge and the radioselenium went in solution and
thus got separated from the copper backing.
A correction for the radiochemical yield was not
necessary since only relative measurements were done.
.Experimental data were obtained via 254-keV gamma-
line.
.Experimental data were obtained via growth and
decay of Se-73-g.
Fig.6 from Phys.Rev.,C38(1988)645
SD: STATUS added.
Thin samples were prepared by electrolytic deposition
of Ge on copper backing.
-By  authors.  In the case of HE3 induced process, only
the effective cross section could be obtained due to
the contribution of several reactions.
Measurement of Se-73-m in alpha particle induced
reactions on Ge presented difficulty due to the  strong
copper matrix activity.  After irradiation each
electroplated foil was therefore treated with 3
milliliters warm 3% hydrogen peroxide.  The very thin
layer of Ge and the radioselenium went in solution and
thus got separated from the copper backing.
A correction for the radiochemical yield was not
necessary since only relative measurements were done.
Fig.7 from Phys.Rev.,C38(1988)645
SD: STATUS added.
Several stacks were irradiated  for 20 minutes
at 50 nanoampere with 45 MeV protons
Thin samples were prepared by electrolytic deposition
of As on Cu or Al backing.
-By authors. The details on the measurement of the
7.1-hr Se-73-g activity have already been described.

-By compiler. Experimental data which are shown on
figure 3 contain a little differences from A0467003.
Therefore data were scanned again.

chemical separation.
